title: Matcha Panna Cotta
ingredients: Heavy cream whipped with powdered sugar for decoration
instructions: If unmolding the panna cotta, lightly grease the molds or ramekins with vegetable oil and wipe out any excess.
Soak the gelatin sheets in cold water in a bread loaf pan until soft and set aside.
Combine milk, cream, sugar, and matcha powder in a medium saucepan and heat over medium heat, stirring frequently until simmering. Do not boil.
Remove from heat. Squeeze gelatin sheets to remove excess water, add to the pan, and stir constantly until the gelatin is melted.
Strain the mixture through a fine sieve and pour evenly among the ramekins or molds.
Refrigerate until set, about 4 hours or more.
If removing from molds, dip the bottom of the mold into a pot of hot water for 5 seconds to loosen, slide a knife around the edge, and invert onto the serving plate.
Serve with freshly whipped cream and berries.
notes: Do not let the matcha mixture boil or it may affect the flavor and texture.
Panna cotta can be served in the ramekins or unmolded onto plates as preferred.
